reviews.review_msg: If you're weak at econ, taking it with Gordon Myers is a total nightmare. Lectures are boring and incomprehensible, the assigned textbook isn't all that helpful, and your mark is entirely made up of two midterms and a final exam. Make sure to buy the optional study guide, as that helps with studying for exams. He killed my interest in econ :(

Gordon himself was not a bad prof at all. He taught his lectures just like any other prof but it could make you fall asleep sometimes because he rambles about unrelated topics sometimes. The main issue I have is his marking scheme. He will not give partial marks at all. It's always a huge surprise when exam marks are released.

3 exams make up your mark. Exams and grading schemes are curved. Terrible lectures will put you to sleep. Not understanding and hard to get a marking error fixed. Don't bother with textbook and don't bother with lectures. Review his lecture slides and watch youtube videos. Tutorial questions are similar to exam questions. Need to go to tuts for ans

ECON103 is pretty straight forward with Myers. I would recommend skipping lectures and reading the textbook. GO TO YOUR TUTORIALS. 

To do well on exams, make sure you understand your tutorial questions, then do the end of chapter textbook questions. Just doing that for each chapter got me an A-.

Unless you buy the study guide, you probably won't do that well (unless you have A LOT of time to study). The class was based on three exams. Even if you think you understand the questions on the exams and you've got the right answers, you don't. His lectures aren't useful because he uses a lot of personal examples that don't help you understand.

Econ 103 is definitely a difficult course - with Myers - keep up with the lecture slides and tutorial questions each week. Exams were very similar to tutorial questions. Even though he's monotone, he's a very caring prof and has a lot of knowledge about economics. It can be difficult to follow him  but puts a lot of effort into the course.

Grab a blanket! myers rambles a lot about his own life+achievents in a slow monotone voice, if you did not get much sleep at night, you'll be sure to doze off in class. Classwise, he doesnt cover all the material so you must read the textbook on your own. He likes to reword the exam questions so make sure you understand content conceptually.

I did bad in this class because I have a difficult time with economics. I think prof Myers is ok, his lectures are not helpful at all. Try and utilize outside resources if you struggle with economics.

Took the class in spring 2021. Entirely test based with the grades coming from 3 midterms and the final. Great leactures and easy tests since the practice problems are identical to the test. Only major issue were some tech difficulties during the 3rd final midterm but the prof allowed students to choose if they wanted to void that midterm.
